content.text: Answer four problems. (If you turn in more, the first four will be graded.) Within reason, you may quote theorems as long as you state them clearly.

problem.text: Give an example of each, with a few words of explanation.

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: A finite nilpotent group which is not a \(p\)-group.

subparts.label: (d)
subparts.text: A finite group which is solvable but not nilpotent.

problem.text: Consider the symmetric group \(S_6\), and the corresponding alternating group \(A_6\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: How many elements of order 4 are there in \(S_6\)? Justify your answer.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: How many elements of order 4 are there in \(A_6\)? Justify your answer.

problem.text: Let \(G\) be a finite \(p\)-group and suppose that \(G\) acts on a finite set \(S\). Denote by \(S^G\) the set of all elements of \(S\) fixed by every element of \(G\). Show that \(|S| \equiv |S^G| \pmod p\), where \(|A|\) denotes the cardinality of the set \(A\).
Let \(G\) be a group of order \(32 \cdot 11 \cdot 41\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Show that every 41-Sylow subgroup of \(G\) is normal.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Show that if \(H\) is a group of order \(32 \cdot 11\) then every 11-Sylow subgroup of \(H\) is normal.

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: Show that \(G\) has a normal cyclic subgroup of order \(451 = 11 \cdot 41\).

problem.text: Let \(G\) be a group and let \(g \in G\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Define what is meant by the order of \(g\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Suppose that \(g\) has infinite order, and suppose that \(N\) is a finite normal subgroup of \(G\). Prove, from your definition, that the element \(gN \in G/N\) has infinite order.

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: Suppose that \(g\) has finite order, suppose that \(N\) is a finite normal subgroup of \(G\), and suppose that \(|N|\) is relatively prime to the order of \(g\). Prove, from your definition, that the element \(gN \in G/N\) has the same order as the element \(g \in G\).

problem.text: Show that for any \(n \geq 5\), the alternating group \(A_n\) is the only nontrivial proper normal subgroup of the symmetric group \(S_n\).
